2605.00412.title: Physically Native World Models: A Hamiltonian Perspective on Generative World Modeling
2605.00412.paper_summary: World models have recently re-emerged as a central paradigm for embodied intelligence, robotics, autonomous driving, and model-based reinforcement learning. However, current world model research is often dominated by three partially separated routes: 2D video-generative models that emphasize visual future synthesis, 3D scene-centric models that emphasize spatial reconstruction, and JEPA-like latent models that emphasize abstract predictive representations. While each route has made important progress, they still struggle to provide physically reliable, action-controllable, and long-horizon stable predictions for embodied decision making. In this paper, we argue that the bottleneck of world models is no longer only whether they can generate realistic futures, but whether those futures are physically meaningful and useful for action. We propose \emph{Hamiltonian World Models} as a physically grounded perspective on world modeling. The key idea is to encode observations into a structured latent phase space, evolve the latent state through Hamiltonian-inspired dynamics with control, dissipation, and residual terms, decode the predicted trajectory into future observations, and use the resulting rollouts for planning. We discuss how Hamiltonian structure may improve interpretability, data efficiency, and long-horizon stability, while also noting practical challenges in real-world robotic scenes involving friction, contact, non-conservative forces, and deformable objects.
2605.00412.paper_summary_zh: 世界模型最近重新成為具身智慧、機器人技術、自主駕駛和基於模型的強化學習的核心範式。<lb/>然而，目前的世界模型研究往往受限於三條部分獨立的路徑：強調視覺未來合成的2D視頻生成模型、強調空間重建的3D場景中心模型，以及強調抽象預測表示的類JEPA潛在模型。<lb/>雖然每條路徑都取得了重要進展，但它們仍然難以為具身決策提供物理上可靠、可控的行動和長期穩定的預測。<lb/>在本文中，我們認為世界模型的瓶頸不再僅僅是它們是否能生成現實的未來，而是這些未來是否在物理上有意義並對行動有用。<lb/>我們提出\emph{哈密頓世界模型}作為對世界建模的物理基礎觀點。<lb/>關鍵思想是將觀察編碼到結構化的潛在相空間中，通過受哈密頓啟發的動力學進行潛在狀態的演變，並結合控制、耗散和殘差項，將預測的軌跡解碼為未來觀察，並利用生成的展開進行規劃。<lb/>我們討論了哈密頓結構如何改善可解釋性、數據效率和長期穩定性，同時也指出在涉及摩擦、接觸、非保守力和可變形物體的現實世界機器人場景中存在的實際挑戰。
2605.00412.author: Sen Cui et.al.
